Monday.label: Breakfast
Monday.meal: Oats, banana & peanut butter with whole milk
recipe: Measure 100g rolled oats into a small saucepan and pour in 300ml whole milk.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and stir constantly as the mixture begins to warm, preventing the oats from sticking to the bottom.
Cook for 4-5 minutes, continuing to stir, until the oats have absorbed most of the milk and the porridge reaches a thick, creamy consistency.
Remove from heat and pour into a bowl. Peel and slice the banana into rounds and arrange on top of the oats.
Add 2 tbsp peanut butter — either dolloped in the centre or spread across the top — and serve immediately while hot.

Monday.label: Lunch
recipe: Rinse 150g white rice under cold water, then cook in a pot with 300ml salted water over medium heat for 12-13 minutes until all water is absorbed. Remove from heat, cover and let steam for 5 minutes.
Pat the 180g chicken breast dry with a paper towel and season generously on both sides with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the chicken breast and cook undisturbed for 6-7 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip the chicken and cook a further 6-7 minutes until cooked through (internal temperature 75°C). Remove from heat and rest for 3 minutes before slicing.
Halve the avocado, remove the stone, and slice the flesh into thin strips.
Spoon the cooked rice into a bowl, lay the sliced chicken over the top, and fan the avocado slices alongside. Add a pinch of salt and pepper to finish.

Monday.label: Dinner
recipe: Finely dice the onion and mince the 2 garlic cloves. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and sauté the onion for 5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add 200g ground beef to the pan, breaking it apart with a wooden spoon. Cook over medium-high heat for 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is fully browned with no pink remaining.

Pour in the can of crushed tomatoes, add 1 tsp dried oregano, season generously with salt and pepper, and stir to combine. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil and cook 200g pasta according to packet instructions until al dente. Reserve a splash of pasta water before draining.

Drain the pasta and add directly to the bolognese sauce, tossing well to coat — add a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick. Plate up and finish with a generous grating of parmesan.

Tuesday.label: Breakfast
Tuesday.meal: Greek yogurt with granola, honey & banana
recipe: Spoon 250g Greek yogurt into a wide bowl and spread it out evenly with the back of the spoon.
Scatter 60g granola evenly over the yogurt, covering the surface for maximum crunch in every bite.
Peel the banana and slice it into thin rounds, then arrange the slices over the granola.
Drizzle 1 tbsp honey in a zigzag pattern across the top and serve immediately before the granola softens.

Tuesday.label: Lunch
recipe: Rinse 150g white rice and cook in 300ml salted water over medium heat for 12-13 minutes. Once absorbed, remove from heat and cover to steam for 5 minutes.
Season 180g chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides. Heat a griddle pan or skillet over high heat until very hot.
Cook the chicken on the griddle for 6-7 minutes per side until deep grill marks form and the chicken is cooked through to 75°C internally. Rest for 3 minutes then slice.
While the chicken rests, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add 100g spinach or kale and toss with tongs for 2-3 minutes until wilted.
Squeeze half a lemon over the greens, season with salt and pepper, and toss once more.
Plate the rice, lay the sliced chicken alongside, and spoon the greens next to it. Add an extra squeeze of lemon to finish.

Tuesday.label: Pre-Football
recipe: Place 2 slices of whole grain bread in the toaster and toast to your desired level — medium is ideal for a firm base that holds the toppings.
While the bread is still warm, spread 1 tbsp peanut butter generously across each slice, allowing it to melt slightly into the toast.
Peel the banana and slice into thin diagonal rounds for maximum coverage.
Lay the banana slices across both slices of toast, overlapping slightly. Serve immediately.

Tuesday.label: Recovery
recipe: Cook 150g white rice in 300ml salted water over medium heat for 12-13 minutes, then cover and steam off heat for 5 minutes.
Cut 150g broccoli into evenly sized florets and steam over a pot of boiling water for 5-6 minutes until bright green and just tender. Do not overcook.
Pat the 200g salmon fillet dry and season the flesh side with salt, pepper and dried dill.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat. Place the salmon skin-side down and press gently with a spatula for the first 30 seconds to prevent curling.
Cook skin-side down for 4 minutes until the skin is crispy, then flip and cook a further 3-4 minutes until the flesh is just opaque throughout.
Plate the rice, place the salmon on top skin-side up to keep it crispy, arrange the broccoli alongside, and squeeze fresh lemon juice over everything.

Wednesday.label: Breakfast
Wednesday.meal: Overnight oats with berries, honey & milk
recipe: The evening before: add 100g rolled oats and 1 tbsp chia seeds to a jar or airtight container.
Pour 300ml whole milk over the oats and chia seeds and stir well to combine, ensuring no dry oats remain at the bottom.
Seal the jar and place in the refrigerator overnight, or for a minimum of 6 hours, to allow the oats and chia seeds to fully absorb the milk.
In the morning, remove the jar from the fridge and give the oats a stir — if the mixture seems too thick, loosen with a splash of extra milk.
Top with 100g mixed berries and drizzle 1 tbsp honey over the surface. Serve cold directly from the jar.

Wednesday.label: Lunch
Wednesday.meal: Tuna pasta with olive oil & cherry tomatoes
recipe: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil and cook 180g pasta according to packet instructions until al dente. Reserve a cup of pasta cooking water before draining.
While the pasta cooks, halve 100g cherry tomatoes and thinly slice the garlic clove.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a wide frying pan over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and cook gently for 1-2 minutes until fragrant but not coloured.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and cook for 3-4 minutes, pressing them lightly with the back of a spoon until they begin to burst and release their juices.
Drain the tuna from the can and add to the pan. Break the tuna into flakes and stir gently to combine with the tomatoes.

Drain the pasta and add to the pan, tossing everything together over medium heat. Add a splash of reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce if needed. Season with salt and pepper, tear fresh basil leaves over the top and serve.

Wednesday.label: Post-Gym
recipe: Preheat oven to 200°C. Scrub 200g sweet potato, cut into wedges or rounds, toss with a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt, and spread on a baking tray. Roast for 28-30 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and tender.

Remove the 250g ribeye from the fridge 20 minutes before cooking to bring it to room temperature. Pat dry with paper towel and season very generously with salt, pepper and finely chopped fresh rosemary on both sides.
Heat a heavy cast-iron skillet over the highest heat until it begins to smoke. Add 1 tbsp olive oil and immediately place the steak in the pan.

Sear undisturbed for 3-4 minutes until a deep brown crust forms, then flip. Add 1 tbsp butter and 1-2 smashed garlic cloves to the pan and baste the steak continuously with the foaming butter for 3-4 minutes for medium-rare.
Remove the steak from the pan and rest on a board for at least 5 minutes — this is essential to retain the juices.

While the steak rests, add 100g spinach to the same pan over medium heat, toss with a splash of olive oil and a minced garlic clove for 1-2 minutes until wilted. Slice the steak against the grain and serve alongside the sweet potato and spinach.

Thursday.label: Breakfast
Thursday.meal: Banana smoothie with oats, milk & protein
recipe: Peel 2 bananas — for an extra thick, colder smoothie, use bananas that have been sliced and frozen overnight.
Add the bananas to a blender along with 50g rolled oats. Let the oats sit with a splash of the milk for 1-2 minutes so they soften slightly and blend more smoothly.
Add 1 scoop protein powder, 1 tbsp peanut butter and pour in 300ml whole milk.
Blend on high speed for 45-60 seconds until completely smooth and creamy. If too thick, add a splash more milk and blend again briefly.
Pour into a large glass and drink immediately for best texture.

Thursday.label: Lunch
Thursday.meal: Chicken rice bowl with roasted veg
recipe: Preheat oven to 200°C. Dice the zucchini and bell pepper into roughly 2cm chunks and spread on a baking tray.
Drizzle the veg with olive oil, season with salt, pepper and a generous pinch of paprika, and toss to coat evenly. Roast for 20-22 minutes until the edges are lightly charred.
While the veg roasts, cook 150g rice in 300ml salted water over medium heat for 12-13 minutes. Cover and steam off heat for 5 minutes.
Season 180g chicken breast with salt, pepper and paprika. Heat a dry griddle or skillet over high heat and cook the chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through. Rest for 3 minutes.
Slice the chicken and plate over the cooked rice with the roasted veg spooned alongside.

Thursday.label: Pre-Football
Thursday.meal: Rice cakes with peanut butter & honey
recipe: Lay 4 rice cakes flat on a plate or clean surface.
Using a butter knife, spread approximately half a tablespoon of peanut butter evenly across each rice cake, going all the way to the edges.
Drizzle honey over all four rice cakes — approximately a quarter tablespoon each — in a thin zigzag pattern.
Serve immediately and eat within 30 minutes for best pre-training energy.

Thursday.label: Recovery
recipe: Cook 150g white rice in 300ml salted water over medium heat for 12-13 minutes, then cover and steam for 5 minutes off heat.
Mince 2 garlic cloves finely. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add the garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add 200g ground turkey to the pan, breaking it apart with a spoon. Cook over high heat for 6-8 minutes, stirring often, until fully browned and any liquid has evaporated.
Add 100g mixed veg (carrots, peas, corn) directly to the pan and stir to combine. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the vegetables are heated through.
Splash in 1-2 tbsp soy sauce, stir well and cook for a further minute. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve the turkey and veg mixture over the cooked rice.

Friday.label: Breakfast
Friday.meal: Avocado toast with smoked salmon & olive oil
ingredients: Lemon juice, salt, pepper, chili flakes
recipe: Place 2 slices of sourdough bread in a toaster and toast until deep golden and crisp — sourdough benefits from a slightly longer toast time than regular bread.
Halve the avocado, remove the stone, and scoop the flesh into a small bowl. Add a good squeeze of fresh lemon juice, a pinch of salt and a few grinds of black pepper.
Mash the avocado with a fork to your preferred consistency — slightly chunky gives better texture than completely smooth.
Spread the mashed avocado generously over both slices of toast, pressing it down to create an even layer.
Drape 100g smoked salmon over the avocado, dividing it evenly between the two slices. Drizzle 1 tbsp olive oil over the top.
Finish with a pinch of chili flakes and an extra squeeze of lemon juice. Serve immediately.

Friday.label: Lunch
recipe: If cooking the chicken fresh, season 180g chicken breast with salt and pepper and grill on a hot griddle pan for 6-7 minutes per side. Rest for 3 minutes, then slice into thin strips.
Warm the tortilla wrap in a dry pan over medium heat for 30 seconds per side, or microwave for 15 seconds to make it pliable and prevent tearing.
Spread 1 tbsp Greek yogurt across the centre of the wrap, leaving a 3cm border around the edges.
Layer a handful of mixed salad leaves over the yogurt, then lay the sliced chicken strips on top. Slice the avocado half into thin strips and lay over the chicken.
Season with salt, pepper and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice over the filling.
Fold in the sides of the wrap, then roll firmly from the bottom up, tucking the filling tightly as you go. Cut in half diagonally and serve.

Friday.label: Dinner
recipe: Preheat your oven to 220°C (fan 200°C) and place a baking tray or pizza stone inside to heat up — this helps the base crisp from underneath.
Season 150g chicken breast with salt and pepper and cook in a hot pan with a drizzle of oil for 5-6 minutes per side. Rest briefly, then dice or slice into bite-sized pieces.
Thinly slice the bell pepper and red onion into strips. Halve or leave the black olives whole depending on preference. Tear or roughly slice 100g mozzarella.
Spread 100g tomato sauce evenly over the pizza base, leaving a 1-2cm crust border. Scatter the chicken, pepper, red onion and olives evenly across the sauce.
Distribute the mozzarella over the toppings, drizzle with a little olive oil, and sprinkle dried oregano generously over the whole pizza.
Transfer the pizza to the preheated tray or stone and bake for 12-15 minutes until the crust is golden, the edges are bubbling, and the cheese is melted and beginning to colour.

Saturday.label: Breakfast
Saturday.meal: Pancakes with banana, maple syrup & milk
recipe: Add 150g pancake mix to a mixing bowl and make a well in the centre. Pour in 200ml whole milk gradually, whisking as you go, until the batter is smooth with no lumps. Let it rest for 5 minutes.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium heat and add a small knob of the butter, swirling to coat the surface evenly.

Once the butter is foaming, pour in a ladleful of batter (roughly 80ml) and tilt the pan to spread it into a circle. Cook for about 2 minutes until bubbles form on the surface and the underside is golden.
Flip the pancake carefully and cook for a further 1-2 minutes until the second side is evenly browned. Transfer to a warm plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
Peel and slice 2 bananas into rounds. Stack the pancakes 3-4 high, layer banana slices between and on top, and drizzle 2 tbsp maple syrup over the whole stack.

Saturday.label: Lunch
Saturday.meal: Grilled chicken salad with feta & olive oil
recipe: Season 180g chicken breast generously with salt, pepper and a pinch of dried oregano on both sides. Heat a griddle pan over high heat until smoking.
Cook the chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until deep grill lines form and the meat is fully cooked through. Rest on a board for 3 minutes, then slice diagonally.
While the chicken rests, halve 100g cherry tomatoes and slice the cucumber into half-moons. Place in a large salad bowl with a generous handful of mixed salad leaves.
Crumble 80g feta cheese over the salad in large chunks — avoid breaking it too fine so it stays chunky throughout.
Add the sliced chicken on top. Drizzle 2 tbsp olive oil and a good squeeze of lemon juice over the entire salad.
Season with salt, pepper and a pinch of dried oregano. Toss gently at the table just before eating to avoid wilting the leaves.

Saturday.label: Post-Gym
Saturday.meal: Beef burger with sweet potato fries
recipe: Preheat oven to 200°C. Peel the large sweet potato and cut into evenly sized fries about 1cm thick. Toss in 1 tbsp olive oil and a generous pinch of salt, spread in a single layer on a baking tray and bake for 25 minutes, flipping once at the halfway mark, until crispy on the edges.

While fries are in the oven, season 200g beef mince with salt, pepper and garlic powder. Mix briefly — do not overwork the meat or the burger will be dense — then shape into a patty slightly wider than your bun, as it will shrink during cooking.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or heavy pan over high heat until very hot. Place the patty in the dry pan and press down firmly with a spatula. Cook for 3-4 minutes without moving until a dark crust forms.
Flip once and cook for a further 3-4 minutes for medium, or 4-5 minutes for well done. Add a slice of cheese in the last minute if desired.
Slice the burger bun and lightly toast the cut sides in the residual heat of the pan for 30-45 seconds.
Assemble the burger with the patty on the bottom bun, topped with lettuce, tomato and sliced onion. Serve immediately with the sweet potato fries alongside.

Sunday.label: Brunch
recipe: Combine 150g pancake mix and 200ml whole milk in a bowl and whisk until smooth. Allow the batter to rest for 5 minutes while you prepare the fruit.
Hull and halve the strawberries, leaving the blueberries whole. Set the fruit aside in a bowl.

Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat with a small knob of butter. Pour a ladleful of batter (roughly 80ml) into the pan and cook for about 2 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges look set.
Flip and cook for a further 1-2 minutes until golden underneath. Stack on a warm plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
Spoon 150g Greek yogurt into a bowl or alongside the pancakes. Drizzle 1 tbsp honey over the yogurt and top with the mixed fruit.
Serve the pancakes with the yogurt and fruit on the side, using the yogurt as a dipping sauce or spooning it directly on top.

Sunday.label: Lunch
recipe: Preheat oven to 200°C. Cut 300g potatoes into quarters or chunks of equal size to ensure even cooking. Dice the zucchini and bell pepper into similar-sized pieces.
Smash 3-4 garlic cloves (unpeeled is fine) and pick a few sprigs of fresh rosemary. Combine the potatoes, zucchini, bell pepper, garlic and rosemary on a large roasting tray.
Drizzle 1 tbsp olive oil over the vegetables, season generously with salt and pepper, and toss everything together until well coated. Push the veg to the sides, creating space in the centre.

Pat 300g chicken thighs dry with a paper towel and rub with the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Season all sides well with salt, pepper and additional rosemary, then place skin-side up in the centre of the tray.

Roast in the preheated oven for 38-42 minutes, until the chicken skin is deeply golden and crisp and the potatoes are tender and caramelised at the edges. The chicken should reach an internal temperature of 75°C.
Remove from the oven, let rest for 5 minutes, then serve the chicken thighs over the roasted vegetables, spooning over any pan juices.

Sunday.label: Dinner
recipe: Peel and dice the carrot into small cubes, finely dice the onion, and slice the celery stalks into thin pieces. Aim for uniform sizes so everything cooks evenly.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and celery and sauté for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onion is soft and translucent.
Add the diced carrot and cook for a further 3 minutes, stirring to coat in the oil.
Pour in 800ml vegetable broth, drain and rinse the can of chickpeas and add to the pot, then add 1 tsp dried thyme. Stir to combine and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover partially with a lid, and simmer for 20-25 minutes until the carrots are fully tender and the broth is well flavoured. Taste and season with salt and pepper.
Toast 2 slices of whole grain bread until golden. Ladle the soup into deep bowls and serve with the toast alongside for dipping.
